subject: Get more from Claude with these power moves (5/5)
body:
Easy techniques that can transform your experience from good to great. 




 <https://claude.ai?utm_content=image_link> 



Getting better results with Claude 




 <https://claude.ai?utm_content=image_link> 



As you get comfortable with Claude, here are 4 easy techniques that can 
transform your experience from good to great. 

Chat with Claude  <https://claude.ai?utm_content=hero_cta> 



Ask Claude to think out loud 
When you need help with something complex, try: “Think through this 
step-by-step” or “Break this down into smaller steps.” 


 <https://claude.ai?utm_content=image_link> 



 <https://claude.ai?utm_content=image_link> 

Ask Claude to think out loud 
When you need help with something complex, try: “Think through this 
step-by-step” or “Break this down into smaller steps.” 


Be specific when results are too vague 
If Claude's response isn't quite what you need, add more detail: “That's 
helpful, but I need ideas specifically for a family vacation with teenagers, 
not young children.” 


 <https://claude.ai?utm_content=image_link> 



 <https://claude.ai?utm_content=image_link> 

Be specific when results are too vague 
If Claude's response isn't quite what you need, add more detail: “That's 
helpful, but I need ideas specifically for a family vacation with teenagers, 
not young children.” 



Refine through conversation 
Treat Claude as your creative partner and shape the output together: “I like 
where this email draft is going, could you make it more casual and half as 
long?” 


 <https://claude.ai?utm_content=image_link> 



 <https://claude.ai?utm_content=image_link> 

Refine through conversation 
Treat Claude as your creative partner and shape the output together: “I like 
where this email draft is going, could you make it more casual and half as 
long?” 



Specify your audience and tone 
“Explain investing to a teenager” will get very different results than just 
“Explain investing.”
